Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The log shows main sequence RA to VF to AE to optional loop AD to SC to AC

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also note that in case 2 there is no RMI loop So loop is optional and can repeat

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So process tree sequence of RA VF AE loop AD SC AC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In our case after RMI they re validate form VF then maybe again missing info

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: So after AE we have loop RMI VF

Name: Validation - IND
Text: That matches observed RMI then VF then maybe RMI again

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: In case 3 there are two RMI before VF

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Our loop definition only allows one RMI then optionally VF then RMI again

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: To capture two RMI before VF we could have loop where A RMI and B tau then after loop exit we have VF

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: But simpler treat loop as RMI VF

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The extra RMI can be considered as part of same loop iteration maybe they request missing info twice before validation

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For simplicity we can model loop as RMI VF and accept that it approximates behavior

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus final process tree sequence RA VF AE loop RMI VF AD SC AC
